text.tspan: Understanding the End of Life Option Act
HOW TO FIND OUT MORE
WHAT’S IMPORTANT TO KNOW?
Understanding the Act helps patients and
families make informed choices about end-
of-life care. Knowing this law exists allows
individuals to explore all available options
so they can make the decision that aligns
best with their values, beliefs, and comfort.
WHAT IS THE END OF LIFE OPTION ACT?
WHO QUALIFIES?
An individual must:
WHY WAS THE ACT CREATED?
The End of Life Option Act is a California
law that allows terminally ill adults to
request and obtain a prescription for
medication that they can choose to take to
end their life peacefully. The Act is also
referred to as “medical aid in dying.”
Awareness also helps facilitate open,
compassionate discussions with healthcare
providers and loved ones about wishes for
care at the end of life.
I provide consultations for people interested in learning more
about the End of Life Option Act, even if they are not
terminally ill and do not qualify at the present time. A
referral from a health care provider is not necessary. To
schedule an appointment, please contact me by phone or
email.
Be at least 18 years old
Be a California resident
Have a terminal illness with a
prognosis of six months or less to
live, confirmed by two physicians
Have the mental capacity to make
medical decisions
Make two oral requests at least 48
hours apart and one written request
witnessed by two people
Be able to self-administer the
medication
Geoffrey Weinstein, MD
Geoffrey.Weinstein.MD
@gmail.com
The Act was created to give terminally ill
people greater autonomy and control at the end
of life. It provides patients the option to decide
when and how they wish to bring closure to
their life, in a way that feels most comfortable
and meaningful to them.
